title: System and dielectric antenna with convex dielectric radome
abstract: Aspects of the subject disclosure may include, for example, a dielectric antenna and a convex dielectric radome having an operating face that radiates or receives microwave signals. The operating face is shaped to reduce an accumulation of water on the operating face.
claims: Claims What is claimed is: 1. A communication device, comprising: a dielectric antenna including a convex dielectric radome and a feed point having an end; a cable comprising a dielectric core coupled to the feed point of the dielectric antenna via a joint configured to create a gap between the end of the feed point and an end of the dielectric core and wherein the end of the feed point and the end of the dielectric core are partially misaligned; and a transmitter, coupled to the cable, the transmitter facilitating a transmission of first electromagnetic waves, the first electromagnetic waves guided by the dielectric core, without requiring an electrical return path, to the feed point of the dielectric antenna; wherein the dielectric antenna radiates free-space wireless signals from an aperture of the dielectric antenna in response to the first electromagnetic waves guided by the dielectric core to the feed point of the dielectric antenna; and wherein the convex dielectric radome has an elliptical shape, wherein the free-space wireless signals include near-field wireless signals emitted by an operating face of the dielectric antenna having a first beam pattern cross section of a first elliptical shape and the free-space wireless signals further include far-field wireless signals having a second beam pattern cross section of a second elliptical shape that is rotationally offset from the first elliptical shape. 2. The communication device of claim 1, wherein the convex dielectric radome includes an operating face that is shaped to reduce an accumulation of water on the operating face. 3. The communication device of claim 1, wherein the convex dielectric radome includes an operating face having a hydrophobic coating to reduce an accumulation of water on the operating face. 4. The communication device of claim 1, wherein the convex dielectric radome is composed of a hydrophobic polymer. 5. The communication device of claim 1, wherein the convex dielectric radome comprises a hydrophobic polymer. 6. The communication device of claim 1, wherein the convex dielectric radome includes an operating face that is shaped as an antenna lens. 7. The communication device of claim 6, wherein the antenna lens adjusts a propagation of the first electromagnetic waves in the dielectric antenna to reduce a beamwidth of the free- space wireless signals radiated by the dielectric antenna. 8. The communication device of claim 1, wherein the convex dielectric radome comprises a dielectric material having a first dielectric constant that is substantially similar or equal to a second dielectric constant of the dielectric antenna. 9. The communication device of claim 1, wherein the convex dielectric radome is an integral part of the dielectric antenna. 10. The communication device of claim 1, wherein the convex dielectric radome is coupled to the dielectric antenna. 11. The communication device of claim 1, further comprising a receiver coupled to the cable, the receiver facilitating a reception of second electromagnetic waves associated with wireless signals received by the dielectric antenna. 12. The communication device of claim 1, wherein the first elliptical shape of the near-field wireless signals has a first width-to-height ratio and the second elliptical shape of the far-field wireless signals has a second width-to-height ratio. 13. The communication device of claim 12, wherein the first width-to-height ratio of the near-field wireless signals is inversely proportional to the second width-to-height ratio of the far-field wireless signals. 14. The communication device of claim 1, wherein the dielectric core is resistant to propagation of the first electromagnetic waves having an optical frequency range. 15. A method compri sing : facilitating, via a transmitter, a transmission of first electromagnetic waves, the first electromagnetic waves guided by a dielectric core of a cable to a feed point of a dielectric antenna without requiring an electrical return path, wherein the dielectric antenna includes a convex dielectric radome and the feed point has an end, wherein the dielectric core is coupled to the feed point of the dielectric antenna via a joint configured to create a gap between the end of the feed point and an end of the dielectric core and wherein the end of the feed point and the end of the dielectric core are partially misaligned; and radiating, via the dielectric antenna, free-space wireless signals from an aperture of the dielectric antenna in response to the first electromagnetic waves guided by the dielectric core to the feed point of the dielectric antenna, wherein the convex dielectric radome has an elliptical shape, wherein the free-space wireless signals include near-field wireless signals emitted by an operating face of the dielectric antenna having a first beam pattern cross section of a first elliptical shape and the free-space wireless signals further include far-field wireless signals having a second beam pattern cross section of a second elliptical shape that is rotationally offset from the first elliptical shape.
